results.title: Fix supabase auth bugs in nextjs project
results.description:
I have a nextjs app (using App router) with Supabase backend. I'm having some auth issues. I'll send a video of the issues to a short list of candidates, but at a high level: 

1. I would like a "SupabaseProvider" and SesssionProvider in the main layout page to (as I understand) have easy access to the supabase client and the active session in either client pages or server pages.
2. I have a reset password and change-password pages set up but there is a bug when changing the password where I get an error "Auth Session Missing!". 
3. There seems to be a discrepancy between my useUser hook and supabase.auth.getSession(). I have situtations in testing where there is no session but I still have user details saved. 
4. When the app is working, I was finding I'd be logged out (no session) every hour. I THINK this is a supabase free tier limitation, but please confirm if there any solutions (maybe get a new session before it expires??). 

Must-haves in the fixes: 
- continue using the App router, not pages router
- continue using the supabase-ssr package (not the auth-helpers pkg)
- use typescript
- as much as possible, develop solutions that don't congest the network (that is, if possible, I'd rather get user details once, not on every component)

The end solution I'm looking for is fixes to the bugs above and explain how it works so I understand in the future.

I believe this is a quick couple fixes for people that know what they are doing. If you are an expert in supabase/auth/nextjs, please apply and let's get you some quick money 👍

results.title: TypeScript/Node.js Developer for Chatbot PDF Signature Feature
results.description:
Title: TypeScript/Node.js Developer for Chatbot PDF Signature Feature

Description:

We're seeking a TypeScript and Node.js developer to add a PDF signing feature to our chatbot. This role involves creating a system where agents can mark PDFs for client signatures, automatically fill these PDFs with client-provided details (e.g., name, address), and enable clients to sign in specific areas within the chat interface.

**Requirements:**

- Proficiency in TypeScript and Node.js.
- Experience with PDF manipulation and electronic signature integration.
- Familiarity with chatbot development and seamless integration of new features.
- Strong problem-solving skills, attention to detail, and a focus on security and privacy.

**Project Goals:**

- Enable direct PDF signing within the chat, improving user experience.
- Automate document filling and signing process for efficiency.
- Ensure scalability and adaptability to various PDF documents and signing requirements.

Candidates should demonstrate expertise in web development, PDF handling, and ideally, chatbot enhancement, along with examples of past work.

results.title: Full Stack Developer (React / Node)
results.description:
We are hiring a full-stack developer to help us build and scale our web app.  You’ll be part of a cross-functional team responsible for the full software development life cycle, from conception to deployment.

You should be comfortable around both front-end (React) and back-end (Node) development, development frameworks and third-party libraries. You should also be a team player with a knack for visual design and utility.

Although we see the value that agencies provide, for this position, we prefer to work with an individual contractor that wants to join our team for an extended period of time. If things work out, this could be a longlasting and rewarding relation.

Technology Stack:

React
MaterialUI
NodeJS
graphQL
AWS DynamoDB
AWS Appsync
AWS Lambda
GitLab CI/CD
Sentry

Responsibilities:

- Work with development teams and product managers to ideate software solutions
- Design client-side and server-side architecture
- Build the front-end of applications through appealing visual design
- Develop and manage well-functioning databases and applications
- Write effective APIs
- Test software to ensure responsiveness and efficiency
- Troubleshoot, debug and upgrade software

Requirements:

- Good spoken and written English
- Ability to join and participate on camera in our sprint and other meeting
- Be able to work async and provide communication through the provided communication tools
- Willing to participate in a paid coding test (5-10h)	

Remote Work

At Simply Bread, we are big advocates for building remote and distributed development teams. We treat our remote team members with the utmost respect and integrity and include them in our team to the best of our ability. We also expect our remote team members to act with the same integrity and be fully part of our team. 

About Simply Bread:

We exist to empower the artisan bread-baking community with products and services that are designed to elevate an emerging generation of bakers.

Artisan Bread consumption (and making) is enjoying a renaissance as more and more people are rediscovering the taste of fresh, locally produced artisan bread. 

Simply Bread is hyper-focused on building an active customer community of artisan bakers who want to elevate and monetize their craft. Our flagship product is a baking oven that allows micro-bakeries to create 15 loaves of artisan bread in 40 minutes. We also build an online platform that helps community bakers connect with customers and helps manage their business.

www.simply-bread.co

results.title: Modernise nuxt.js website with Ruby on Rails, Sanity, Redis + ElasticSearch
results.description:
Our client has an existing web app built on the the technology outlined below, that they are wanting to refresh and completely modernize the look of. We have in-house designers who will work with Figma to provide you the front-end design / layout (although they will collaborate with you of course).

They are on a strict deadline of 3-4 weeks, so the first part of the engagement will be to review the details and existing site structure and contents to confirm if this is deliverable in the proposed timeframe or whether we will need additional team members. Note that none of the core site logic, framework or integrations should need to be rebuilt, however if there are benefits to be gained by changing any of the technologies in use, at minimal effort, please do advise us. 

Below is what we expect is required for the front-end development: 

Design and Build
» Setup new build using the latest release, install recommended modules for optimal
website performance such as SEO and speed
» Create a bespoke website theme and templates based on designed created. Implementation of
website functions based on current website sitemap and general website functionality as per
brief (mobile support)
» Setup website hosting containers for Staging and Production
» Liaise with the client for DNS changes
» Install and configure SSL certificates
» Cross-device testing, Performance testing and Review of project
» All standard features as listed
» CSS & HTML multi-level page development
» UX design input into page development CMS system setup and full content
loading » Creation of test site under agency hosting
» Refinement based on testing and client feedback (allowance for two rounds)

Below is the current stack in use:

Frontend

Main framework used is Nuxt.js. In addition, following are also used to help manage data display on the front side

Core
NodeJS - Node version used to develop the frontend / NuxtJS.
Sanity (CMS) 
Google Map / API - This is used as Any Property's main street viewer tool to feed and display properties data.
Backend

API backend framework is built with Ruby on Rails. 
Redis
Sidekiq - Background processor for Ruby on Rails
Ruby on Rails - Main backend API framework
ElasticSearch - A java server that filters properties data with post-calculated scores.

Data import and staging (you may not need to lead this, but guidance and input will be appreciated).

We will be given a large flat file (table format) to be imported into the CMS to hold the application data which is 'listings'. In addition, media (images) will also need to be able to be imported in bulk to be associated with the various 'listings'

A bonus would be experience in AWS services provisioning and migrating from another AWS instance to a new one.

Please let us know if there are any questions.

Screenshots of current website (which is offline) will be provided to the shortlisted candidates for final estimation of effort

results.title: Vue.js Developer Needed to Fix Chatbot Widget Interruption Bug
results.description:
Job Description:

We are looking for a skilled Vue.js developer with experience in web-based chatbot applications to fix a specific bug in our chatbot widget. Our chatbot is designed to provide audio responses to user queries, but we've encountered an issue that needs to be resolved to improve user experience.

Video of Bug here: https://icecreamapps.com/v/dxzv6yg

Issue Description:

Currently, when the chatbot is in the process of reading out an answer, if a user tries to interrupt by clicking on the microphone button to ask another question, the chatbot does not stop the previous answer. Instead, it restarts the previous answer from the beginning once the new question is processed and answered. This creates a frustrating experience for users who wish to quickly move on to different questions without waiting for the previous answer to complete.

Desired Outcome:

We want the chatbot to be able to handle interruptions gracefully. Specifically, when a user clicks the microphone button while an answer is being read out:

The current audio response should stop immediately.
The chatbot should listen for the new question.
After receiving the new question, the chatbot should process it and start reading out the new answer without restarting the previous one.
Requirements:

Proven experience with Vue.js and creating or working with web-based chatbot applications.
Ability to troubleshoot and debug complex issues efficiently.
Experience with handling audio playback and interruptions in web applications.
Excellent communication skills and ability to provide updates on progress.

results.title: [$500] IOU - Report fields are blank after refresh or leave from report #35772 - Expensify
results.description:
Expensify is a team of generalists developing today's leading expense management tool. Maintaining our reputation as an innovative leader in the world of finance requires an incredibly reliable and secure system for processing financial transactions. Accordingly, we primarily leverage time-tested languages, but we're looking to unify our front-end across platforms. For this, we're leveraging React Native and are looking toward the community on Upwork to help solve a variety of problems, both big and small, related to this migration.

Please see the GitHub issue for full details: github.com/Expensify/App/issues/35772

Your Proposal:

- You *must* post a proposal for how you will fix this issue in the GitHub issue linked above.
- Your proposal should include a technical explanation of the changes you will make. You are not required to submit the final solution or code along with your proposal.
- Your proposal will be reviewed.
- If your proposal is accepted, you should post the proposal in Upwork and you will be hired for the job.
- AFTER your proposal is accepted in Upwork and you have accepted the offer, you may submit the code to implement your solution. To submit the code, go to the Expensify/App GitHub repo where you'll create a fork of our codebase. You'll create a branch on that fork, and when your code is ready for review, you'll create a pull request in our repository to merge your code into our codebase. Include screenshots and confirmation that you have tested the pull request on all platforms. Our engineers will review the code, and approve and merge when the code meets our requirements.

Please review our Contributor Guidelines before submitting a proposal - github.com/Expensify/App/blob/main/contributingGuides/CONTRIBUTING.md

**Important:** As documented in our CONTRIBUTING.md, payment amounts are variable, dependent on any regressions your work causes.

results.title: Website built on python 2.7 / vue.js: Improvements, Maintenance & New features to be implemented.
results.description:
Hi there, we are looking for someone to help us maintain and develop the following platform: www.headup.space (a free storytelling platform available worldwide since 2016 to provide a service to anyone out there who needs to communicate). We need to implement new features, get ideas suggestions, especially now with free AI tools available, bots... 
This is a short-term work but could become a longer-term partnership with you.
Here is the repo for reference, have a look and if you are passionate about the idea and already see some improvements to make this project bigger, let us know: https://github.com/tabaresjc/HeadUP

Some first features we need:
- Better image resizing for users
- Add time reading feature on the stories (cf Hackernoon)
- Add tags to segment the stories (serving as filters) allowing search + alphabetical searches and by date.
- Recreate a share button for both desktop and mobile to help users to push their content to any other platform (wechat, Line, whatsapp, reddit, linkedin, facebook, insta, etc)
- Add cool AI features
- Update logo
- Update URLs using Headline of the story
- Add a section on the website for people to create their own blog or make their stories visible to only a restricted group of people
- Others

Happy to discuss that over a call and decide for a first short collaboration to see how it goes. Thanks a lot! 

PS: We want passionate people about the project as this is our project for the global community to empower anyone who wants to share her/his story to the rest of the world.
